explanation: This LTL property expresses the following logic: Whenever either PCVMode or PSVMode is active and the inspiratoryPauseButton (set by the GUI) is pressed, at the next end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the inspiratoryPause is still required, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves (inValveOpen and outValveOpen) should be closed (i.e., both !inValveOpen and !outValveOpen) until the pause is stopped. For this translation, we formalize the obedience to Inspiratory Pause request during PCV/PSV modes, ensuring the transition to both valves closed after the next inspiration if the pause remains set by the GUI. This property is future-looking: After the inspiratoryPhaseEnd and while the button is set, both valves remain closed until otherwise changed (pause stopped). We choose template 10 ("G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)") to express: Whenever the relevant conditions (bool_exp2: (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) hold, then the required safety condition (bool_exp3: (!inValveOpen & !outValveOpen & inspiratoryPauseButton)) must hold globally. N_DURATION is not required in this template.

This LTL property states: Whenever the system is in PCV or PSV mode AND the Inspiratory Pause has been set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), after the next occurrence of the end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the Inspiratory Pause is still active, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves should be closed (i.e., inValveOpen and outValveOpen are both false) for as long as inspiratoryPause is true. This is captured using the template for 'Whenever bool_exp2 holds, bool_exp3 shall globally hold': bool_exp2 = (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton & inspiratoryPhaseEnd (which models the moment just after the next inspiration is finished, when a pause may be required), and bool_exp3 = (inspiratoryPause -> (!inValveOpen & !outValveOpen)) (valves must close if and only if inspiratory pause is intended). This captures the requirement that, after the next inspiration ends while the pause is requested, and for as long as the inspiratory pause remains set, the valves are closed.

The requirement describes a conditional mode: whenever the inspiratory pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then, after the next inspiratory phase ends, if the pause is still needed (inspiratoryPause remains true), both the inspiratory and expiratory valves shall remain closed (inValveClose & outValveClose) until the pause has ended (inspiratoryPause = false). Among the provided LTL templates, Template 17 is the closest fit, expressing that whenever the inspiratoryPause is active, valves must stay closed for the entire duration. Here, 'inspiratoryPause' serves as the condition (bool_exp2), requiring (bool_exp3) to be true as long as the pause is. This captures: whenever inspiratoryPause is active, inValveClose and outValveClose must be true (closed).

This LTL property expresses that whenever either PCVMode or PSVMode is active and the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton is true), then, globally, after the end of the next inspiration phase (indicated by inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the Inspiratory Pause is still required (inspiratoryPause is true), both the inspiratory and expiratory valves remain closed (inValveOpen and outValveOpen are both false), until the Inspiratory Pause is stopped by the GUI. This is modeled by triggering (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton, and enforcing globally that (inspiratoryPause -> (!inValveOpen & !outValveOpen)). No use of bool_exp2 is necessary in this precise template (template 17 is most appropriate).

This LTL property states: Globally (always), when the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton) and the system is in either PCVMode or PSVMode, then at the end of the next inspiration phase (i.e., right after inspiratoryPhaseEnd), both the inspiratory and expiratory valves will be closed (i.e., inValveOpen is false and outValveOpen is false) as long as the pause button remains active. The LTL property is encoded so that whenever inspiratoryPauseButton & (PCVMode | PSVMode) becomes true, after the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, we expect (!inValveOpen & !outValveOpen) to hold, as long as inspiratoryPauseButton is true. This matches the requirement: 'When Inspiratory Pause is set, at the end of the next inspiration, if still required, close the valves until pause stopped.'

The property checks that whenever the Inspiratory Pause is requested via the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then after the next inspiration phase ends (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the Inspiratory Pause is still active (inspiratoryPause), then both the inspiratory and expiratory valves are closed (i.e., !inValveOpen & outValveClose), and remain so until the GUI stops the pause. The template uses bool_exp1 as inspiratoryPauseButton (pausing requested by the user), bool_exp2 as inspiratoryPhaseEnd (wait for the next inspiration to finish), and bool_exp3 as (inspiratoryPause & (!inValveOpen & outValveClose)) (i.e., the pause is actively holding both valves closed). We select template 41 to ensure after pause is requested and inspiration ends, if pause is still required, the correct valve state is enforced immediately.

This LTL property formalizes the core logic required for the Inspiratory Pause behavior as described. When either PCV or PSV mode is selected (PCVModeSelected or PSVModeSelected), if the GUI requests an inspiratory pause (inspiratoryPauseButton), after the end of the next inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), and as long as the inspiratory pause is still required (inspiratoryPause), both valves should close (inValveClose and outValveClose), maintaining this state until the GUI stops the inspiratory pause (i.e., inspiratoryPause is no longer true). This mapping uses bool_exp1 = (PCVModeSelected | PSVModeSelected), bool_exp2 = (inspiratoryPauseButton), bool_exp3 = ((inValveClose & outValveClose) & inspiratoryPause), and N_DURATION = 1. The LTL template ensures that in the relevant modes, whenever the inspiratory pause is requested and becomes active after the inspiration ends, the valves remain closed until the pause is cancelled, which matches the stakeholder requirements on ventilator pause logic.

This LTL property encodes the requirement for PSV and PCV modes to allow an inspiratory pause triggered by the GUI, which must be executed at the end of the next inspiration. The property states: Whenever (1) we are in either PCV Mode or PSV Mode, and (2) the user has requested an inspiratory pause via the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then (3) after the next end of the inspiratory phase (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if the pause is still required (i.e., inspiratoryPause remains true), both inspiratory and expiratory valves should close (inValveOpen is false and outValveOpen is false), and this state should persist until the GUI removes the pause (i.e., inspiratoryPauseButton is removed, which would clear inspiratoryPause). Thus, the formula ensures the system can respond appropriately to an inspiratory pause request. N_DURATION is set to 2 as a reasonable fixed value representing 'at the end of the next inspiration.'

This LTL property expresses that: always, if the ventilator is in either PCV or PSV mode and the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (inspiratoryPauseButton), then after the next end of inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), at the next time step, both the inspiratory and expiratory valves must be closed (inValveClose and outValveClose). The property encodes the causality and conditional timing from the requirement: as soon as the pause is requested (while in an appropriate mode), and the next inspiratory phase ends, then both valves must be closed (start inspiratory pause). This template does not express holding the pause as long as the pause button is held, but it correctly captures the triggering behavior described as required after the next inspiration phase end once paused is set.

The requirement states that when in PCV or PSV mode, if the user sets Inspiratory Pause via the GUI, then at the end of the current inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is still required, the ventilator must close both inspiratory and expiratory valves (i.e., both inValveClose and outValveClose are true) until the pause is stopped by the GUI.

This property is best captured as: When (1) in PCV or PSV mode and (2) the inspiratoryPauseButton is set (i.e., by the GUI), then at the next end of inspiration (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), if inspiratoryPause is still required, then inValveClose and outValveClose must be true (valves closed). This must hold globally as a mode-of-operation property. 

bool_exp1: ( (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton )                           # System is in correct mode and Inspiratory Pause set by GUI
bool_exp2: inspiratoryPhaseEnd                                                      # The next end of inspiration
bool_exp3: ( inspiratoryPause & inValveClose & outValveClose )                      # During pause, both valves are closed

Template 31 encodes that, globally, whenever (PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPauseButton becomes true, then at the next inspiratoryPhaseEnd, the system will enter and maintain the inspiratoryPause condition (where both valves are closed and pause is ongoing).

N_DURATION is not needed for this template (uses 'X' after the trigger event).
